LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF DID NOT GIVE NOTICE THAT HE WILL CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT ALLOWED ARGUMENTS. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION 5-11 IN PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT DEMONSTRATES THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT COMPLIED WITH THE CLAIM FILING REQUIREMENTS OF THE GOVERNMENT CLAIMS ACT PRIOR TO FILING THE COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO PLEAD THAT HE HAS MET THE REQUIREMENTS OF THE GOVERNMENT CLAIMS ACT, INCLUDING GOVERNMENT CODE SECTIONS 910 AND SECTION 915(E). SEE DICAMPLI-MINTZ V. COUNTY OF SANTA CLARA (2012) 55 CAL 4TH 983, 991-992. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED 30 DAYS TO FILE AND SERVE THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. ELECTRONIC SERVICE IS AUTHORIZED WHEN A PARTY HAS AGREED TO ACCEPT SERVICE ELECTRONICALLY IN THAT ACTION. (CCP 1010.6 (A)(2).) A PARTY INDICATES THAT THE PARTY AGREES TO ACCEPT ELECTRONIC SERVICE BY SERVING A NOTICE ON ALL PARTIES THAT THE PARTY ACCEPTS ELECTRONIC SERVICE AND FILING THE NOTICE WITH THE COURT, OR BY ELECTRONICALLY FILING ANY DOCUMENT WITH THE COURT. MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Franklin Wright, 415.716.0898, plaintiff in propria persona; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HOST HOTELS & RESORTS, INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is granted. (CCP 436.) The demurrer is untimely. "A party who has filed a complaint or cross-complaint may, within 10 days after service of the answer to his pleading, demur to the answer." (CCP 430.40(b).) On March 18, 2014, Defendant Host Hotels & Resorts, filed and mail-served its Answer. On April 15, 2014, Plaintiff filed his demurrer to the Answer, which is untimely. Plaintiff served the demurrer by email, which is improper absent the parties' agreement. (CCP 1010.6.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Demurrer to Causes of Action 5-11 in Plaintiff's Complaint is sustained with leave to amend.
